subject: Human Resources
title: AI and Bias in HR Technology
description: A quick, practical guide to AI and Bias in HR Technology.

hook.text: Amazon built an AI recruiting tool. It penalized resumes containing the word 'women's.' They scrapped it.

buildup.text: AI tools screen resumes, score candidates, and even conduct video interviews—all trained on historical data.

discovery.text: If historical data reflects past bias—fewer women hired, fewer minorities promoted—AI learns and amplifies that bias.

twist.text: AI feels objective. But algorithms are only as fair as the data and decisions they're trained on—garbage in, bias out.

climax.text: Ethical AI requires diverse training data, bias audits, human oversight, and transparency about how decisions are made.

punchline.text: AI doesn't eliminate bias. It scales it—unless you actively prevent it.

quiz.question: Why did Amazon's AI recruiting tool fail?
options: It learned bias from historical data and penalized women's resumes
